tests/expected/general--www.figma.com-blog-introducing-codex-to-figma.md:40:After iterating in code, you can bring your design back into the canvas to compare flows, explore alternatives, and validate your assumptions. The Figma MCP server makes it easy to bring those screens back into Figma without having to recreate them manually from the browser. The rendering process follows a predictable sequence. First, the widget's configuration is validated against its schema. Then, the layout engine calculates the widget's dimensions based on its container and siblings. Finally, the paint phase converts the abstract layout into visible elements on screen.

tests/fixtures/comments--news.ycombinator.com-item-id=12345678.html:141: tests/expected/general--x.com-article-2026-02-13.md:18:the markdown files know everything ive discovered, nicely structured and with automatic situational context injection for in-context learning tests/expected/general--x.com-article-2026-02-13.md:24:the deeper thing is that a vault encodes how you think, not just what you thought about. the methodology becomes part of the system its all just markdown files, you own it completely. this is ai as thinking partner, not as a writing assistant tests/expected/general--x.com-article-2026-02-13.md:38:a vault is a folder of markdown files that link to each other: tests/expected/general--x.com-article-2026-02-13.md:40:```markdown tests/expected/general--x.com-article-2026-02-13.md:106:1. markdown files with links that any ai can read tests/expected/general--x.com-article-2026-02-13.md:141:```markdown tests/expected/general--x.com-article-2026-02-13.md:166:3. topic pages (MOCs) that link to related notes these act like tables of contents for each subject tests/expected/general--x.com-article-2026-02-13.md:196:```markdown tests/expected/general--x.com-article-2026-02-13.md:213:markdown is the system. tools like obsidian are just windows into it. the vault could survive any app disappearing tests/expected/general--x.com-article-2026-02-13.md:232:- a vault is just markdown files that link to each other tests/expected/general--x.com-article-2026-02-13.md:236:- what stays constant: markdown, links, ai operates while you provide judgment tests/fixtures/general--react-streaming-ssr.html:27:

The rendering process follows a predictable sequence. First, the widget's configuration is validated against its schema. Then, the layout engine calculates the widget's dimensions based on its container and siblings. Finally, the paint phase converts the abstract layout into visible elements on screen.

content.text: I found likely paths in the parser/removal pipeline and tests. Next I’ll inspect the core pipeline, removals, standardization, markdown, and fixture test harness to decide where to add a temporary regression test and the fix.

I found table conversion in `src/markdown.ts` and empty-element cleanup in `src/standardize.ts`; next I’ll add a temporary focused test that demonstrates the empty-cell table currently leaks into markdown.

content.content:
1	{
2		"name": "defuddle",
3		"version": "0.16.0",
4		"description": "Extract article content and metadata from web pages.",
5		"main": "dist/index.js",
6		"bin": {
7			"defuddle": "dist/cli.js"
8		},
9		"module": "dist/index.js",
10		"types": "dist/index.d.ts",
11		"typesVersions": {
12			"*": {
13				"*": [
14					"dist/index.d.ts"
15				],
16				"full": [
17					"dist/index.full.d.ts"
18				],
19				"node": [
20					"dist/node.d.ts"
21				]
22			}
23		},
24		"exports": {
25			".": {
26				"types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
27				"import": "./dist/index.js",
28				"require": "./dist/index.js"
29			},
30			"./full": {
31				"types": "./dist/index.full.d.ts",
32				"import": "./dist/index.full.js",
33				"require": "./dist/index.full.js"
34			},
35			"./node": {
36				"types": "./dist/node.d.ts",
37				"import": "./dist/node.js"
38			}
39		},
40		"scripts": {
41			"clean": "rm -rf dist",
42			"build:types": "tsc --project tsconfig.declarations.json",
43			"build:js": "webpack",
44			"build:node": "tsc --project tsconfig.node.json",
45			"build": "npm run clean && npm run build:types && npm run build:node && npm run build:js",
46			"prepublishOnly": "npm run build",
47			"dev:types": "tsc --project tsconfig.declarations.json --watch",
48			"dev:js": "webpack --watch --mode development",
49			"dev:node": "tsc --project tsconfig.node.json --watch",
50			"dev": "npm run clean && npm run build:types && concurrently \"npm run dev:types\" \"npm run dev:node\" \"npm run dev:js\"",
51			"test": "TZ=UTC vitest run",
52			"test:jsdom": "TZ=UTC DOM=jsdom vitest run",
53			"playground": "node playground/server.js"
54		},
55		"keywords": [
56			"readability",
57			"content-extraction",
58			"article-extraction",
59			"web-scraping",
60			"html-cleanup",
61			"content-parser",
62			"article-parser",
63			"dom"
64		],
65		"author": "kepano",
66		"license": "MIT",
67		"repository": {
68			"type": "git",
69			"url": "git+https://github.com/kepano/defuddle.git"
70		},
71		"bugs": {
72			"url": "https://github.com/kepano/defuddle/issues"
73		},
74		"homepage": "https://github.com/kepano/defuddle",
75		"dependencies": {
76			"commander": "^12.1.0"
77		},
78		"optionalDependencies": {
79			"linkedom": "^0.18.12",
80			"mathml-to-latex": "^1.5.0",
81			"temml": "^0.13.1",
82			"turndown": "^7.2.0"
83		},
84		"devDependencies": {
85			"@types/jsdom": "^21.1.6",
86			"@types/node": "^20.19.0",
87			"@types/turndown": "^5.0.5",
88			"concurrently": "^8.2.2",
89			"jsdom": "^24.0.0",
90			"terser-webpack-plugin": "^5.3.14",
91			"ts-loader": "^9.5.1",
92			"typescript": "^5.3.3",

content.text:
Implemented the fix in `src/standardize.ts`.

What changed:
- `removeEmptyElements()` now treats a `<table>` with one or more `td`/`th` cells and zero meaningful text content as empty, so it gets removed during standardization.
- This strips visual/progress/layout tables like Wikisource’s `pr_quality` table before markdown conversion can emit an empty table.

Validation:
- Added a temporary regression test for the `pr_quality`-style empty-cell table.
- Confirmed it failed before the fix.
- Applied the fix.
- Confirmed the temporary regression test passed.
- Removed the temporary test as requested.
- Re-ran existing `tests/markdown.test.ts`: all 8 tests passed.

No commits were made.
